WASHINGTON. March THE re-
port of Charles ]. Bonaparte, special
commissioner to investigate the
charges made against the administra-
tion of Indian Territory affairs, was
filed with congress this afternoon. He
finds the conditions involve imminent
danger of wrong doing to the general
Indian population which would be a
profound discredit to the United
States. He demands drastic remedies
and recommends that the Indian COM
mission be abolished. He also recon-
mends that agents be kept independ-
ent of each other and that the admin
istration of affairs In the territory be
committed to officers on the spot,
carefully superintended but not con-
trolled from Washington. Special
allegations respecting the federal Of
true.


He finds that Tams Bixby, chairman
of the Dawes commission. was inter
ested in a real estate transaction In
the territory to the prejudice of the
best interests of the Indians. c. II.
breckenridge, Thomas Needles, a. l..
Aylesworth chief clerk of the com
mission, and ii. V. Smith. dlsbursing
agent, are found also to have been
connected I'm these operations. He
recommended that the practice be for
hidden and that the federal officials
connected Herewith be separated
from office. Secretary Hitchcock APP
proves the report.
